findings.title: listAll query does unbounded collect() — WQ-087 still open
findings.description: The `listAll` query (backing `useAllSyncedLogs`) calls `.collect()` with no limit. For a long-term user this will scan every log row on every subscription tick. The work queue already tracks this as WQ-087 (incorrectly marked done, then reopened per the 2026-03-21 audit). The query is called from `SyncedLogsContext` which wraps the entire app, so it fires for every authenticated page view.
findings.fix: Add a hard ceiling (e.g. 10 000) and paginate, OR restructure so the context only fetches the window callers actually need. At minimum add `.take(N)` until pagination is implemented.

findings.title: count query does an unbounded collect() to count rows
findings.description: The `count` query fetches every row just to return `rows.length`. Convex has no native COUNT aggregate, but collecting all documents just to count them wastes both bandwidth and time proportional to the user's history. This will degrade as data grows.
findings.fix: Use a Convex aggregate (if available) or store a denormalized count document that is incremented/decremented by the `add` / `remove` mutations. If neither option is feasible, at minimum document the limitation and gate use to admin/debugging contexts only — do not call it in production UI code.

findings.title: Date.now() used inside Convex mutation payload — non-determinism risk
findings.description: `useAddUserMessage` and `useAddAssistantMessage` both pass `timestamp: Date.now()` directly to a Convex mutation. This is called from the client, so it is not inside a mutation handler (it is not technically non-determinism in the Convex replay sense), but the timestamp is generated on the client clock, which may be wrong, skewed, or manipulated by the user. Any time-sorted query over conversation messages will produce inconsistent results if two clients or two rapid calls produce colliding or out-of-order timestamps. The standard Convex pattern is to let the server generate the timestamp inside the mutation handler.
findings.fix: Remove `timestamp` from the client payload and generate it inside the Convex mutation handler using `Date.now()` (which is allowed inside Convex mutations — it is deterministic within a single execution). This closes the client-clock manipulation vector.

findings.title: Raw ctx.auth.getUserIdentity() used instead of requireAuth — legacy pattern not yet migrated
findings.description: All handlers in `convex/logs.ts` (and the other Convex files inspected) call `ctx.auth.getUserIdentity()` directly and then `if (identity === null) throw new Error("Not authenticated")`. The CLAUDE.md and auth ADR specify that `requireAuth(ctx)` from `convex/lib/auth.ts` is the canonical pattern. The raw pattern is the legacy form flagged as HIGH — 60+ instances still remain. This file is the single biggest source of that debt since it is called on virtually every user action.
findings.fix: Replace each `const identity = await ctx.auth.getUserIdentity(); if (identity === null) throw ...` block with `const { userId } = await requireAuth(ctx);` from `convex/lib/auth.ts`. This is mechanical and safe to do in a single PR.

findings.title: Migration calls patchProfile before deleting the legacy blob — partial-failure window
findings.description: If `patchProfile` succeeds but `del(LEGACY_KEY)` fails, the migration will not re-run (because `raw` is truthy on the next run), meaning the legacy blob lives forever but won't cause harm. However, if `patchProfile` throws, the migration exits with an unhandled rejection and the legacy blob is never deleted. On the next app load, the migration runs again, re-patching the same fields (idempotent for Convex `upsert`, but not proven so for all callers), and re-calling `setApiKey` with the same key (harmless). This is probably safe, but the logic flow should be documented. More critically, there is no caller-visible error if `patchProfile` throws — the `await patchProfile(updates)` is not inside a try/catch, so it will propagate as an unhandled promise rejection and the function will exit without deleting the blob.
findings.fix: Wrap `patchProfile(updates)` in a try/catch. On failure, log a clear error and return false (do not attempt deletion). This makes the failure observable and keeps the blob for retry.

findings.title: API key from legacy storage is written without validation
findings.description: `data.openAiApiKey` is written to the new `apiKeyStore` via `setApiKey()` if it is a non-empty string. There is no format validation (e.g. `sk-` prefix check). A corrupted or injected key would silently propagate to the new store and then be sent to the OpenAI API on the next AI call, producing a confusing auth error rather than a clear migration-time warning.
findings.fix: Add a basic format check (e.g. `data.openAiApiKey.startsWith('sk-')`) before writing. Log a warning and skip if invalid. This is a one-time migration path so the cost is low.

findings.title: All fluid types share the same calendarDaySpan denominator — averages are deflated when new fluid types are added
findings.description: The `fluidCalendarDays` divisor is computed from the span of ALL fluid log timestamps. If a user has been logging water for 90 days but only started logging tea 5 days ago, tea's `avgMlPerDay` is divided by 90 (not 5), making it look like they barely drink tea. The per-habit section correctly uses a per-habit span; the fluid section does not.
findings.fix: Compute a per-fluid-name calendarDaySpan from the timestamps of logs that contain that fluid item, in the same way per-habit spans are computed. Replace the shared `fluidCalendarDays` with per-fluid denominators when populating `fluidBaselines`.

findings.title: normalizeDigestiveCategory throws on out-of-range bristolCode from untrusted data
findings.description: `normalizeDigestiveCategory` passes `Number(data?.bristolCode)` directly to `bristolToConsistency`, which throws if the code is outside 1-7. The function is declared to accept `Record<string, unknown>`, meaning it can be called with external/legacy data containing Bristol codes outside the 1-7 range (e.g. 0, 8, or a float). A throw inside a data-mapping function can crash the analysis pipeline rather than skipping the bad record.
findings.fix: Check the range before calling `bristolToConsistency`: `if (Number.isFinite(code) && code >= 1 && code <= 7)`. This check already exists in `baselineAverages.ts` (line 255) for the same data — apply it here too for consistency.

findings.title: useSetIngredientOverride passes canonicalName unsanitized to the mutation
findings.description: The `canonicalName` and `note` fields are passed to the `ingredientOverrides.upsert` mutation. The `note` field is optional and also not sanitized. The `canonicalName` is a lookup key used throughout the analysis pipeline. If it contains control characters or excess whitespace, it could silently mismatch against normalized names in the pipeline. All other string inputs in the sync layer go through `sanitizePlainText` or `sanitizeUnknownStringsDeep` before being sent to Convex.
findings.fix: Wrap the call in `upsert({ canonicalName: sanitizePlainText(canonicalName, { preserveNewlines: false }), status, ...(note !== undefined ? { note: sanitizePlainText(note) } : {}) })`.

findings.title: useClearIngredientOverride passes canonicalName unsanitized
findings.description: Same pattern as SSL-009. `remove({ canonicalName })` is called with the raw caller-supplied string. A mismatched canonical name (e.g. trailing space) would silently no-op the remove rather than deleting the override.
findings.fix: Apply `sanitizePlainText(canonicalName, { preserveNewlines: false })` before passing to the mutation.

findings.title: habitId field reused as a generic key field in fluid deltas — misleading type
findings.description: `BaselineDelta` uses the field name `habitId` for both habit deltas and fluid deltas (where it holds a fluid name like `"water"` or the sentinel `"__total_fluid__"`). This is called out with an inline comment (`// use fluidName as the key for fluid deltas`) but the type mismatch will confuse future callers and produces an incorrect schema for the AI analysis that reads these deltas.
findings.fix: Rename the field to `key` or `entityId` in the `BaselineDelta` type. Update all consumers. If the type is shared with Convex validators, check that the validator name also changes.

findings.title: sanitizeUnknownStringsDeep on the weekly summary payload uses default 5000-char limit
findings.description: `useAddWeeklySummary` calls `sanitizeUnknownStringsDeep(payload)` without a custom `maxStringLength`. The `weeklySummary` field could legitimately exceed the default 5000-char `genericStoredString` limit for a verbose LLM-generated weekly summary (the AI analysis uses 50 000). This would throw an unhandled error at the Convex mutation call site.
findings.fix: Pass `{ maxStringLength: INPUT_SAFETY_LIMITS.aiPayloadString }` (as `syncAi.ts` and `syncLogs.ts` do for similar LLM payloads) or define a dedicated limit constant for weekly summaries.

findings.title: Date.now() called at the top of computeBaselineAverages — clock captured before heavy computation
findings.description: `const now = Date.now()` is captured before the rest of the computation. If the computation is slow (large log history), the `computedAt` timestamp in the returned object may be noticeably earlier than when the result is actually used. This is cosmetic for most uses but worth noting for any time-since-computed display logic.
findings.fix: Move `const now = Date.now()` to immediately before the `return` statement.

findings.title: toValidatedSyncedLog casts data without runtime shape validation
findings.description: Each branch of the switch does `data as LogDataMap[K]`, relying entirely on the server-side Convex validator to guarantee data shape. This is architecturally correct, but there is no comment or assertion to guard against future schema changes where the server returns a superset of the client type (e.g. server adds a new field that the client type does not model). If the client type diverges from the server schema, the cast silently succeeds and downstream code may read `undefined` for a field it expects.
findings.fix: Not urgent — the Convex type generation (`_generated/dataModel`) mitigates this. Add a comment noting that correctness relies on `dataModel` staying in sync. Consider adding a narrow runtime assertion on at least one discriminating field per case in a dev build.
